summary: The Docker-based reproduction provisions a fresh Laravel host app, mounts the vulnerable package checkout, overwrites the published `key_model.php` helper through the unauthenticated editor endpoint, and confirms attacker-controlled PHP execution when the documented `/pay/click/1/1000` route loads the modified file.
expected_security_impact: The reproduction should show that an unauthenticated request can replace a published Payment helper with attacker-controlled PHP and then trigger that PHP through a normal payment route, producing the exact proof string `EVOHUNT-491ec345-ARBITRARY-PHP` inside the target application.
actual_observed_result: Running `./reproduction/run.sh` cloned commit `2c5fa0515bdb01b403bf3cacf419afca2738d05b`, built the reproduction image, stood up a temporary Laravel application inside the container, received a success response from `/payment/api/editable/update`, requested `/pay/click/1/1000`, and observed `storage/app/evohunt-proof.txt` containing the exact proof string `EVOHUNT-491ec345-ARBITRARY-PHP`.
verification_method: The verification script confirms that the editor update endpoint returned `responseStatus=success`, the `/pay/click/1/1000` response contained the expected Click payment form URL `https://my.click.uz/pay/`, and the injected `key_model.php` created `storage/app/evohunt-proof.txt` with the exact proof string. This distinguishes attacker-controlled PHP execution from a generic HTTP success, startup issue, or unrelated file creation.
evidence_produced_by_run: The temporary Laravel host app contained `storage/app/evohunt-proof.txt` with the exact contents `EVOHUNT-491ec345-ARBITRARY-PHP` after the `/pay/click/1/1000` request.
The editor overwrite request to `/payment/api/editable/update` returned a success JSON response before the trigger request was sent.
The trigger response contained the Click redirect form URL `https://my.click.uz/pay/`, showing that the normal payment path continued after the injected PHP executed.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
